methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $95M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Hadrian
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.6B

private.name: Nimble Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B

data.narrative:
Vention, a Series C robotics and manufacturing startup founded by Etienne Lacroix and Max Windisch, has raised $95M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 9 same-sector peers at similar or adjacent stages, comprising 8 private companies and 1 public entity. Top comparables include 1X Technologies ($10.0B, Series B), Exotec ($2.0B, Series D), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B, Series B), Dexterity ($1.6B, Series B), and Hadrian ($1.6B, Series C).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. The stage-progression method applies a 1.7x step-up multiplier to the $95M total raised proxy, implying a $162M valuation. The peer-median method references the $1.6B median valuation across the 9-company comparable set, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. This dispersion reflects material differences in stage maturity and market position within the robotics sector.

Synthesizing both approaches yields a triangulated range of $137M (low) to $1.8B (high), with a median of $881M. The stage-progression method anchors the conservative bound, while the peer-median dominates the midpoint and upper range. Key uncertainties include: heterogeneity in peer-stage composition (Series B through D), the wide valuation dispersion across comparables, and whether Vention's capital efficiency and market traction justify positioning toward the peer median versus the stage-progression floor.
